method: Document-class (support) word-length register signature; Kruskal-Wallis global + within-site Mann-Whitney/KW + stratified permutation; LB positive control (planted-difference detection + false-positive rate over 200 null splits).

sensitivity_min_len_2.note: Under min_len=2 the Khania Roundel-vs-Tablet direction FLIPS (Tablet longer), so cross-site direction consistency is NOT robust to dropping single-sign words. Global signal and pooled permutation remain highly significant.

key_findings: Global Kruskal-Wallis across 5 support classes (>=40 words each) is highly significant (H=556.67, p=3.688e-119): document classes DO differ in word length.
PC PASSED: machinery detects a planted LB length difference (p=0.0) and false-positive rate over 200 null splits is 0.065 (<=0.10).
Within-site (held-out) test: 2/3 testable sites show a significant support-length effect (p<=0.05).
At Haghia Triada (Tablet/Nodule/Roundel co-occur): Roundel longest (2.47), Nodule shortest (1.05), p=2.8e-90. At Khania (Tablet/Roundel): Roundel longer than Tablet, p=6.0e-3.
Direction (min_len=1): Roundel is the LONGER support in BOTH significant sites -> consistent direction; pooled stratified permutation p=2.0e-4.

HONEST CAVEAT: the cross-site direction consistency is fragile — under min_len=2 the Khania Roundel-vs-Tablet contrast flips sign (Tablet becomes longer). The global signal and pooled permutation are robust to min_len, but the 'consistent direction' clause rests on single-sign words.

The dominant structural finding is robust: document classes carry distinct word-length distributions that survive site control (significant within-site at Haghia Triada and Khania, pooled permutation p=2e-4). The specific 'Roundel always longer' directionality is the fragile part.
successor_hypotheses: H1: Test the support-length signature on a third independent site by relaxing the >=15-words-per-support threshold (e.g. >=10) to add Phaistos / Knossos Tablet-vs-Clay-vessel, checking if the within-site effect generalizes.

H2: Decompose the length distribution into single-sign vs multi-sign word fractions per support class — is the 'register' signature driven by the proportion of single-sign (possibly logographic/numeric-adjacent) tokens rather than the continuous length distribution?
H3: Co-control for support AND context (LMIB vs other) jointly: does the support-length effect survive when both site and archaeological context are held fixed?
H4: Apply the same within-site machinery to LB document classes (if metadata available) as a cross-script validation of the 'document class carries a length register' claim.
H5: Test whether the support-length signature is monotonic in an administrative 'formality' ordering (Nodule < Tablet < Roundel < Clay vessel < Stone vessel) rather than just pairwise different.
H6: Examine whether the Roundel length elevation is driven by a small set of high-frequency longer formulae (a lexical-formula artifact) vs a genuine distributional shift across the whole vocabulary.
non_circular: Sign/word tokens carry NO phonetic/sound/meaning/language/reading content. Word length = len(signs) of t=='word' stream tokens, a purely structural/administrative measure. Statistics are L2/L3 distributional only (Kruskal-Wallis, Mann-Whitney, permutation). LB is used solely as a positive-control benchmark to validate test calibration (planted-difference detection + false-positive rate); no LB result is interpreted as a reading or meaning. No adjudication by the operator — verdict computed mechanically from the frozen rule in prereg.md.
deviations: PC false-positive estimator refined from 25 to 200 null splits BEFORE any LA verdict was computed (prereg allowed '>=20 random splits'); 25-split estimate was noisy (binomial SD ~0.044). Documented in prereg transparency note. Re-frozen plan_hash after refinement.
Metal object (39 words) excluded from the GLOBAL test per the >=40-word keep rule; it appears in support_classes numbers for completeness but is not in the Kruskal-Wallis.
